config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the select. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the select in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The start address of the first or only register of the Select (can be decimal or hexadecimal). Note that only Holding registers are supported.

config_entries.label: Optionsmap
config_entries.description: Provide a mapping from options (str) of this Select to values (int) of the modbus register and vice versa. All options and all values have to be unique.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Force New Range
config_entries.description: If possible sensors with sequential addresses are grouped together and requested in one range. Setting this to true enforces the start of a new range at that address.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Lambda
config_entries.description: Lambda to be evaluated every update interval to get the current option of the select. Parameters passed into lambda sensor. Note: because the response contains data for all registers in the same range you have to use data[item->offset] to get the first response byte for your sensor. Possible return values for the lambda.

config_entries.label: Optimistic
config_entries.description: Whether to operate in optimistic mode - when in this mode, any command sent to the Modbus Select will immediately update the reported state. Defaults to false. ```yaml.

config_entries.label: Register Count
config_entries.description: The number of registers which are used for this Select. Only required for uncommon response encodings or to optimize modbus communications. Overrides the defaults determined by value_type.

config_entries.label: Skip Updates
config_entries.description: By default, all sensors of a modbus_controller are updated together. For data points that don't change very frequently, updates can be skipped. A value of 5 would only update this sensor range in every 6th update cycle. Note: The modbus_controller groups components by address ranges to reduce number of transactions. All components with the same starting address will be updated in one request. skip_updates applies for all components in the same range.

config_entries.label: Use Write Multiple
config_entries.description: By default the modbus command Function Code 6 (Preset Single Registers) is used for setting the holding register if only one register is set. If your device only supports Function Code 16 (Preset Multiple Registers) set this option to true.

config_entries.label: Value Type
config_entries.description: The datatype of the modbus data. Defaults to U_WORD.

config_entries.label: Write Lambda
config_entries.description: Lambda to be evaluated on every update of the Sensor, before the new value is written to the modbus registers.
